- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""FS-Researcher adaptive query generation for the production retrieval path.
2	
3	I-recency-001 (#1296). FS-Researcher (arXiv 2602.01566) WON the recency-completion query-gen
4	re-bake-off under a positive-control-validated judge — balanced across axes (general drb_72
5	finding_coverage 0.561, clinical 3-slug avg 0.351), 2nd on BOTH and never weak. It SUPERSEDES
6	IterResearch, whose earlier "0.386" win did NOT reproduce on the validated judge (it scored 0.000
7	general / ~0.232 clinical = near-WORST). This wires FS-Researcher's PORTABLE scaffold into the
8	production sweep as the query generator, FLAG-GATED (PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER; default OFF =>
9	byte-identical to the legacy template-facet path).
10	
11	The method (arXiv 2602.01566, primary-source verified): build an index.md table-of-contents by
12	deconstructing the question into sub-topics (a todo queue); for each todo derive ONE search query,
13	retrieve, and fold the result in; then run a FIXED 6-item self-review checklist (exhaustive
14	coverage: 'a question the KB cannot fully answer?'; information density: 'an aspect with only 1-2
15	weak sources?') whose output becomes the next round's deficient todos. Repeat until the checklist
16	reports NONE or the query budget is exhausted.
17	
18	FAITHFULNESS: this changes ONLY which queries are issued. Every query still flows through the
19	UNCHANGED `run_live_retrieval` (scope gate, tier classify, fetch, provenance), and the faithfulness
20	engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance) is never touched. The per-query retrieval
21	results are MERGED (dedup by source URL, evidence ids renumbered globally) into one
22	LiveRetrievalResult so downstream (consolidation -> generation -> verify -> render) sees the same
23	contract as today. Mirrors `iterresearch_query_gen.py` (`merge_retrieval_results` is identical).
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import logging
29	import os
30	import re
31	import time
32	from typing import Any, Callable
33	
34	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.fs_researcher_query_gen")
35	
36	# (research_question, **kw) -> LiveRetrievalResult. Injected so this module never imports the
37	# 1000-line live_retriever at module load (and so it is unit-testable on a stub).
38	PerQueryRetrieveFn = Callable[..., Any]
39	# (prompt) -> text. The GLM-5.2 policy. Injected (async client wrapped to sync by the caller).
40	LlmFn = Callable[[str], str]
41	
42	
43	def fs_researcher_enabled() -> bool:
44	    """True iff the FS-Researcher query-gen path is flag-enabled (default OFF = legacy behaviour)."""
45	    return os.getenv("PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER", "0").strip() in ("1", "true", "True")
46	
47	
48	def _max_queries() -> int:
49	    """Max search queries issued (the equal-budget cap the bake-off used). Caps cost."""
50	    return int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER_MAX_QUERIES", "35"))
51	
52	
53	def _max_rounds() -> int:
54	    """Max outer todo-queue / checklist re-plan rounds."""
55	    return int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER_MAX_ROUNDS", "6"))
56	
57	
58	def _qgen_parallel_workers() -> int:
59	    """I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344): bounded-parallel worker count for the seed-frontier fan-out.
60	
61	    Default 1 = SERIAL, byte-identical to the legacy per-query loop. >1 = a bounded ThreadPool over
62	    ``per_query_retrieve`` with an ORDER-STABLE merge. A compute-safety UP bound (LAW VI env cap
63	    ``PG_QGEN_PARALLEL_QUERIES``), never a breadth target — the SAME query set is issued, only
64	    concurrently. Fixes the measured throughput collapse where a serial per-query retrieval tail hit
65	    the retrieval wall and only ~3 of ~35 planned queries fired. §-1.3: additive orchestration; the
66	    frozen faithfulness engine is untouched. A bad value falls back to 1 (serial)."""
67	    try:
68	        return max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_PARALLEL_QUERIES", "1")))
69	    except ValueError:
70	        return 1
71	
72	
73	def _issue_seed_frontier(
74	    seed_queries: list[str],
75	    seen_q: set[str],
76	    budget: int,
77	    per_query_retrieve: PerQueryRetrieveFn,
78	    retrieve_kwargs: dict,
79	    wall_passed: Callable[[], bool],
80	) -> tuple[list[str], list[Any], list[Any]]:
81	    """Issue up to ``budget`` unique, not-yet-seen seed queries (first-seen-wins, in seed order)
82	    through ``per_query_retrieve`` and return ``(issued_queries, results, corpus_rows)`` in seed order.
83	
84	    ``seen_q`` is updated IN PLACE with exactly the issued queries. Serial + byte-identical to the
85	    legacy loop at ``PG_QGEN_PARALLEL_QUERIES=1``; bounded-parallel (order-stable merge) when >1.
86	
87	    I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344): the parallel path selects the SAME deduped, budget-capped query set
88	    the serial scan would issue, dispatches them concurrently in a bounded ThreadPool, and merges the
89	    results back in seed order — so the issued query set + result order are identical to serial; only
90	    the wall-timing of a mid-frontier trip differs (the parallel dispatch issues the whole batch under
91	    one wall instead of dropping the tail to a serial-loop wall). §-1.3 additive: SAME queries, SAME
92	    ``per_query_retrieve`` chokepoint, SAME per-query result contract; drops zero sources; the frozen
93	    faithfulness engine is untouched.
94	    """
95	    issued: list[str] = []
96	    results: list[Any] = []
97	    corpus_rows: list[Any] = []
98	
99	    workers = _qgen_parallel_workers()
100	    if workers <= 1:
101	        # SERIAL — byte-identical to the legacy per-query loop (wall + budget checked per iteration).
102	        for q in seed_queries:
103	            if len(issued) >= budget or wall_passed():
104	                break
105	            k = q.lower()
106	            if k in seen_q:
107	                continue
108	            seen_q.add(k)
109	            issued.append(q)
110	            result = per_query_retrieve(research_question=q, **retrieve_kwargs)
111	            results.append(result)
112	            corpus_rows.extend(list(getattr(result, "evidence_rows", None) or []))
113	        return issued, results, corpus_rows
114	
115	    # PARALLEL — order-stable. Select the IDENTICAL query set the serial scan would issue (dedup +
116	    # budget), then issue concurrently and merge in seed order. The anti-dark fire marker is emitted at
117	    # EVERY return path below carrying the REALIZED ``issued`` count — I-deepfix-001 Wave-3b (#1344,
118	    # Codex P1.2): the prior single PRE-ISSUE marker logged ``selected=N`` BEFORE ``selected[0]`` was
119	    # issued, so a wall trip on ``selected[0]`` skipped ``_rest`` yet the log still read "fired N"
120	    # (INTENT, not realized ISSUE). ``selected`` is declared up-front so the marker is valid on the
121	    # wall-passed early return too.
122	    selected: list[str] = []
123	
124	    def _emit_fanout_marker() -> None:
125	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3b (#1344): anti-dark liveness marker (Fable/Codex P1) — REALIZED-count
126	        # edition. Emitted ONLY on the >1 (parallel) path so the serial default stays byte-identical AND
127	        # the official run's log can DISTINGUISH serial from parallel-N — the exact seam where the "only
128	        # ~3 of ~35 queries fired" collapse was diagnosed. ``selected=`` is the pre-issue dedup count
129	        # (intent breadcrumb); ``issued=`` is the REALIZED fan-out the canary keys on, so a wall-tripped
130	        # 1-of-N now logs issued=1 (NOT selected=N). issued=0 (wall tripped before any seed /
131	        # all-duplicate / budget-0) is the eligible-yet-zero signal — NEVER gated on >0 (§-1.3: a wall
132	        # truncation is compute-safety, not a source drop). Structural presence + counts; the frozen
133	        # faithfulness engine is untouched.
134	        logger.info(
135	            "[activation] qgen_parallel_fanout: workers=%d selected=%d issued=%d",
136	            workers, len(selected), len(issued),
137	        )
138	
139	    if wall_passed():
140	        _emit_fanout_marker()
141	        return issued, results, corpus_rows
142	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344) — Codex P0 (no-drop / order-stable): dedup the SELECTION against a
143	    # LOCAL key set and mutate the shared ``seen_q`` ONLY as each query is actually ISSUED (mirroring
144	    # the serial loop, which adds to ``seen_q`` immediately before it retrieves). The prior code marked
145	    # EVERY ``selected`` query seen up-front; when the retrieval wall tripped on ``selected[0]`` the
146	    # un-issued ``_rest`` were skipped BUT stayed in ``seen_q``, so the downstream facet-completeness
147	    # expansion loop (which reads ``seen_q`` as ``already_issued``) could never re-issue them — a real
148	    # dropped-source breadth loss. Marking seen only on issue leaves the un-issued tail eligible for
149	    # re-issue, exactly as the serial path leaves it (§-1.3 no drop).
150	    _selected_keys: set[str] = set()
151	    for q in seed_queries:
152	        if len(selected) >= budget:
153	            break
154	        k = q.lower()
155	        if k in seen_q or k in _selected_keys:
156	            continue
157	        _selected_keys.add(k)
158	        selected.append(q)
159	    if not selected:
160	        _emit_fanout_marker()  # realized issued=0 (all-duplicate / budget-0): eligible-yet-zero (§-1.3)
161	        return issued, results, corpus_rows
162	    from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
163	
164	    def _one(q: str) -> Any:
165	        return per_query_retrieve(research_question=q, **retrieve_kwargs)
166	
167	    # RACE-SAFETY at the PRODUCTION call site (Fable P1): the injected ``per_query_retrieve`` closure
168	    # (``_iter_per_query_retrieve`` in run_honest_sweep_r3) attaches the layer-4 DOI ``seed_urls`` to
169	    # the FIRST call it sees via a check-then-set on shared state written for SERIAL calls. Under a
170	    # concurrent first batch several threads would each read ``done=False`` and re-fetch the SAME seed
171	    # PDFs — never DROPS a source (additive-safe, faithfulness-neutral) but burns paid fetches
172	    # nondeterministically and which query carries the seed becomes random. Bind the seed
173	    # DETERMINISTICALLY to the FIRST selected query by issuing it SERIALLY before the fan-out; the
174	    # remaining queries then dispatch in the bounded pool. The parallel path now attaches the seed to
175	    # exactly the query a serial run would (order-stable) — a strict throughput win, no shared-state race.
176	    _first_result = _one(selected[0])
177	    seen_q.add(selected[0].lower())  # Codex P0: mark seen ONLY on actual issue (like the serial loop)
178	    issued.append(selected[0])
179	    results.append(_first_result)
180	    corpus_rows.extend(list(getattr(_first_result, "evidence_rows", None) or []))
181	    _rest = selected[1:]
182	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344) — retrieval-wall re-check (Codex P1): ``selected[0]`` above is
183	    # issued SERIALLY and can itself consume the shared retrieval wall. Mirror the serial loop's
184	    # per-iteration ``wall_passed()`` guard by re-checking BEFORE dispatching the remaining frontier;
185	    # otherwise a wall tripped by ``selected[0]`` would still launch the whole ``_rest`` batch past the
186	    # wall — violating the stated retrieval-wall bound (off-path execution/spend). Skipping ``_rest`` on
187	    # a passed wall is EXACTLY what the serial path does (its next iteration would ``break``), so this
188	    # never issues MORE than serial and coverage stays additive — the wall is the compute-safety bound,
189	    # not a source drop (§-1.3). OFF (workers<=1) is untouched: this branch is parallel-only.
190	    if _rest and not wall_passed():
191	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=min(workers, len(_rest))) as _ex:
192	            _result_list = list(_ex.map(_one, _rest))
193	        for q, result in zip(_rest, _result_list):
194	            seen_q.add(q.lower())  # Codex P0: mark seen ONLY on actual issue (like the serial loop)
195	            issued.append(q)
196	            results.append(result)
197	            corpus_rows.extend(list(getattr(result, "evidence_rows", None) or []))
198	    _emit_fanout_marker()  # realized issued count (serial-first + parallel-rest, or wall-tripped 1-of-N)
199	    return issued, results, corpus_rows
200	
201	
202	def _seed_angles_per_facet() -> int:
203	    """R2 (I-deepfix-001, #1344): how many PRIMARY angle queries per facet the SEED pass
204	    issues when the R2 facet-completeness expansion loop is ON.
205	
206	    The seed pass issues the first ``_seed_angles_per_facet()`` angle(s) of every facet
207	    (breadth-first across ALL facets) and RESERVES each facet's remaining angle queries for
208	    the R2 completeness loop to fire ONLY on the facets the seed corpus leaves UNCOVERED.
209	    This is what makes the expansion loop non-vacuous: the prior code registered EVERY angle
210	    query as a seed, so :func:`facet_completeness.run_facet_expansion` (which draws from the
211	    SAME ``facet.queries``) always read "frontier exhausted" and issued zero expansion
212	    queries (the Codex/Fable P1).
213	
214	    A compute-safety split (seed breadth first, then deepen only the gaps) — never a breadth
215	    target (§-1.3). Default 2. When R2 is OFF the full angle frontier is seeded (no reserve
216	    carve), so the R1-only path is unchanged in query count."""
217	    try:
218	        return max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_EXPERT_FACET_SEED_ANGLES", "2")))
219	    except ValueError:
220	        return 2
221	
222	
223	def _breadth_first_seeds(facets: list[Any], angle_limit: "int | None") -> list[str]:
224	    """Order the facets' angle queries BREADTH-FIRST (angle-major): every facet's angle-0
225	    before any facet's angle-1, and so on.
226	
227	    Spreads the seed query budget across ALL facets first — fixing the measured "only 7 of
228	    35 facets seeded" starvation where a facet-major seed drained the whole budget on the
229	    first few facets and later facets were never queried. When ``angle_limit`` is set (R2
230	    on), only the first ``angle_limit`` angle(s) per facet are seeded so each facet's
231	    remaining angles stay a RESERVE for the R2 expansion loop; ``angle_limit=None`` (R2 off)
232	    seeds the full angle frontier so the R1-only path is unchanged in count. De-duplicates
233	    case-insensitively, order-preserving. Drops ZERO facets (§-1.3 — this only orders and
234	    splits the same query set)."""
235	    max_a = max((len(getattr(f, "queries", []) or []) for f in facets), default=0)
236	    if max_a <= 0:
237	        return []
238	    # Leave >= 1 reserve angle per facet when R2 is on (clamp so a small angle count can
239	    # never accidentally seed every angle and re-starve the expansion loop).
240	    if angle_limit is None:
241	        limit = max_a
242	    else:
243	        limit = min(max(1, angle_limit), max(1, max_a - 1))
244	    seeds: list[str] = []
245	    seen: set[str] = set()
246	    for a in range(min(limit, max_a)):
247	        for f in facets:
248	            qs = getattr(f, "queries", []) or []
249	            if a < len(qs):
250	                q = qs[a]
251	                k = q.lower()
252	                if k not in seen:
253	                    seen.add(k)
254	                    seeds.append(q)
255	    return seeds
256	
257	
258	# R5 (I-deepfix-001, #1344): display names for the target languages, for the
259	# production translator prompt. Code -> human name so the LLM translates reliably.
260	_LANG_DISPLAY: dict[str, str] = {
261	    "zh": "Chinese", "ja": "Japanese", "ko": "Korean", "ru": "Russian",
262	    "ar": "Arabic", "fr": "French", "de": "German", "es": "Spanish",
263	}
264	
265	
266	def _multilingual_native_reserve() -> int:
267	    """R5 (I-deepfix-001, #1344): the minimum number of native-language queries
268	    GUARANTEED a slot within the FS-Researcher query budget on a multilingual task.
269	
270	    Without this, the multilingual additions are appended AFTER every English seed,
271	    so a wide English R1 frontier (e.g. 12 facets x 5 angles = 60 seeds) fills the
272	    whole ``PG_QGEN_FS_RESEARCHER_MAX_QUERIES`` budget and NO native-language query
273	    is ever issued — the corpus stays English-only on a zh task (the Codex/Fable P1).
274	
275	    This is a language-ROUTING guarantee (ensure the task's OWN language is actually
276	    queried), NOT a breadth target (§-1.3): it reorders the SAME query set so a
277	    bounded slice of native queries lands inside the budget; it adds no query and
278	    drops none. It is clamped so it can never displace more than half the English
279	    breadth. Env-driven `PG_MULTILINGUAL_QUERY_RESERVE` (default 6)."""
280	    try:
281	        return max(0, int(os.getenv("PG_MULTILINGUAL_QUERY_RESERVE", "6")))
282	    except ValueError:
283	        return 6
284	
285	
286	def _make_multilingual_translate_fn(
287	    llm: LlmFn,
288	    base_queries: list[str],
289	    non_english_langs: tuple[str, ...],
290	) -> "Callable[[str, str], str] | None":
291	    """Build a bounded, memoized ``(english_query, lang) -> translated_query`` using
292	    the injected GLM policy, so an EXPLICIT-language task ('Answer in Chinese') with
293	    an all-ASCII English body actually emits native-language queries on the production
294	    path (the Codex P1). The question carries no native script there, so a true
295	    translation is the ONLY source of a native query.
296	
297	    Cost is bounded to ONE ``llm`` call per target language (the whole seed batch is
298	    translated in a single call, not one call per query), memoized. Best-effort: any
299	    exception or empty / echo reply yields no translation for that query, and the
300	    module simply skips it — the faithfulness engine is never touched (this only
301	    decides WHICH queries are searched). Returns ``None`` when translation is
302	    unavailable (no ``llm`` / no seed queries / no target language)."""
303	    if llm is None or not base_queries or not non_english_langs:
304	        return None
305	    _cache: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {}
306	
307	    def _batch(lang: str) -> dict[str, str]:
308	        if lang in _cache:
309	            return _cache[lang]
310	        mapping: dict[str, str] = {}
311	        try:
312	            numbered = "\n".join(f"{i + 1}. {q}" for i, q in enumerate(base_queries))

337	def _reserve_native_within_budget(
338	    english_seeds: list[str],
339	    native_additions: list[str],
340	    max_queries: int,
341	) -> list[str]:
342	    """Reorder the seed queries so a bounded slice of native-language queries is
343	    GUARANTEED to fall inside the first ``max_queries`` issued positions.
344	
345	    The R5 expansion appends native queries after every English seed; the seed issue
346	    loop stops at ``max_queries``; so a wide English frontier starves the native
347	    queries out entirely (Codex/Fable P1). This reorders — never drops — the SAME
348	    query set: it keeps an English-breadth head, then the reserved native queries
349	    (so they issue within budget), then the English tail and any leftover native
350	    queries (issued only if budget remains). English-only tasks (empty
351	    ``native_additions``) are byte-identical (returns ``english_seeds`` unchanged).
352	
353	    §-1.3: a language-routing reorder, not a cap/target — every query is preserved;
354	    only issue ORDER changes so the task's own language is actually queried."""
355	    if not native_additions:
356	        return list(english_seeds)
357	    # Never displace more than half the English breadth (both dimensions survive).
358	    reserve = min(len(native_additions), _multilingual_native_reserve(), max(1, max_queries // 2))
359	    if reserve <= 0:
360	        return list(english_seeds) + list(native_additions)
361	    reserved_native = native_additions[:reserve]
362	    leftover_native = native_additions[reserve:]
363	    head_len = max(0, max_queries - reserve)
364	    english_head = english_seeds[:head_len]
365	    english_tail = english_seeds[head_len:]
366	    return english_head + reserved_native + english_tail + leftover_native

419	def _stance_diversify_seeds(facets: list[Any], question: str) -> list[str]:
420	    """Build the GENERAL stance-diversified query frontier for the planned facets.
421	
422	    For each facet, emit one query per generic STANCE frame (supporting / opposing / challenges /
423	    opportunities), each = ``{facet} {stance lens} {question anchor}`` — the facet supplies the
424	    sub-topic, the stance lens supplies the viewpoint, and the anchor (reused from the sibling
425	    expert-facet planner) keeps the query inside the question's subject so it cannot generalise into the
426	    facet's broad field (the same scope-anchor discipline R1/landmark use). Deterministic (no LLM), $0.
427	    Ordered, case-insensitively de-duplicated. Returns ``[]`` when there are no facets.
428	
429	    GENERAL, no benchmark-gaming: the stance lens words are topic-agnostic templates — no study title /
430	    country / benchmark topic is baked in. §-1.3: this ADDS on-topic queries only; it DROPS ZERO sources
431	    and CAPS NOTHING (no target count, no per-facet cap knob — the frontier size emerges from the fixed
432	    four frames x the facets); every emitted query still routes through the UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve
433	    + the frozen faithfulness engine — a stance query that finds nothing verifiable contributes nothing.
434	    """
435	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import expert_facet_planner as _efp
436	    anchor = _efp._question_anchor(question)
437	    out: list[str] = []
438	    seen: set[str] = set()
439	    for f in facets:
440	        name = (getattr(f, "name", "") or "").strip()
441	        if not name:
442	            continue
443	        for _label, lens in _STANCE_FRAMES:
444	            q = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", f"{name} {lens} {anchor}").strip()
445	            if not q:
446	                continue
447	            key = q.lower()
448	            if key in seen:
449	                continue
450	            seen.add(key)
451	            out.append(q)
452	    return out

455	def _lines(text: str, cap: int = 12) -> list[str]:
456	    """Parse an LLM reply into clean sub-topic / query line items (strip numbering/bullets)."""
457	    out: list[str] = []
458	    for raw in (text or "").splitlines():
459	        s = raw.strip()
460	        if not s:
461	            continue
462	        s = re.sub(r"^\s*(?:[-*]|\d+[.):])\s*", "", s).strip().strip('"').strip()
463	        if s and len(s) > 2 and not s.lower().startswith(("here", "sure", "the following")):
464	            out.append(s)
465	        if len(out) >= cap:
466	            break
467	    return out

535	def _plan_expert_facet_queries(
536	    question: str,
537	    llm: LlmFn,
538	    per_query_retrieve: PerQueryRetrieveFn,
539	    *,
540	    max_queries: int | None = None,
541	    retrieve_kwargs: dict | None = None,
542	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: "float | None" = None,
543	    retrieval_plan: Any = None,
544	    retrieval_policy: Any = None,
545	) -> tuple[list[str], list[Any]]:
546	    """R1+R2 (I-deepfix-001, #1344) facet-driven frontier: seed queries from the expert-facet tree,
547	    issue them directly, then (when R2 is enabled) run the facet-completeness expansion loop over the
548	    UNCOVERED facets until the source yield saturates. Returns (queries_issued, per_query_results),
549	    the SAME contract as ``plan_fs_researcher_queries``.
550	
551	    R1 widens the frontier (facet x angle, scope-anchored) — the largest single DRB-II recall lever.
552	    R2 keys the completeness/expansion loop to the TASK's own facets so a general (non-clinical) task
553	    no longer reads a vacuous "0 of 0" and fires real gap-closing retrieval. Both ADD on-topic queries
554	    only and DROP ZERO sources; the FS-Researcher ``max_queries`` cap (compute-safety) still bounds
555	    cost; the R2 saturation stop is yield-keyed, never a breadth count (§-1.3).
556	    """
557	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import expert_facet_planner as _efp
558	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import facet_completeness as _fc
559	
560	    max_queries = max_queries or _max_queries()
561	    retrieve_kwargs = dict(retrieve_kwargs or {})
562	
563	    queries: list[str] = []
564	    results: list[Any] = []
565	    seen_q: set[str] = set()
566	    corpus_rows: list[dict] = []
567	
568	    _deadline = retrieval_deadline_monotonic
569	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344): seconds of wall-time consumed by the ADDITIVE landmark-study
570	    # planning lane, credited BACK to the wall so that lane rides OUTSIDE the baseline retrieval budget
571	    # and never displaces a baseline query (Codex/Fable P1 "or add budget"). A mutable box so the nested
572	    # ``_wall_passed`` reads the running total; it stays 0.0 (byte-identical) unless the landmark lane fires.
573	    _extra_lane_credit = [0.0]
574	
575	    def _wall_passed() -> bool:
576	        _d = _deadline
577	        if _d is not None and _extra_lane_credit[0]:
578	            _d = _d + _extra_lane_credit[0]
579	        return _retrieval_deadline_passed(_d)
580	
581	    if _wall_passed():
582	        return queries, results
583	
584	    # R1: build the facet tree (one bounded LLM call) and its angle queries. The
585	    # contract's SCOPE is carried into the frontier by the projection's amplified
586	    # queries (`_projection_amplified_queries`, prepended below) — NOT by rewriting
587	    # facet query text — so the champion facet frontier stays a byte-identical
588	    # subset and the superset invariant holds. `retrieval_policy` therefore drives
589	    # the NEGATIVE-predicate / backend-filter view (exclusions, date interval,
590	    # quality), never the positive facet query text.
591	    facets = _efp.plan_expert_facets(question, llm)
592	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): expert-facet-planner ACTIVATION fire marker. Emitted ONLY when
593	    # PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER is ON (this whole facet path is reached only under the flag; the guard keeps
594	    # the marker OFF byte-identical even if a test drives this helper directly). facets=0 with the flag ON
595	    # is the eligible-yet-zero (degenerate-LLM-reply) signal. Structural presence + count (§-1.3).
596	    if _efp.expert_facet_enabled():
597	        logger.info("[activation] expert_facet_planner: facets=%d", len(facets))
598	
599	    # R1+R2 seed/reserve split (I-deepfix-001, #1344). Seed BREADTH-FIRST (every facet's
600	    # primary angle before any facet's deeper angle) so the query budget spreads across ALL
601	    # facets — fixing the measured "only 7 of 35 facets seeded" starvation. When the R2
602	    # completeness loop is ON, seed only the first `_seed_angles_per_facet()` angle(s) per
603	    # facet and RESERVE each facet's remaining angles for the R2 expansion loop to fire on
604	    # the facets the seed corpus leaves UNCOVERED. The prior code registered EVERY angle as a
605	    # seed, so `run_facet_expansion` (drawing from the SAME `facet.queries`) always read
606	    # "frontier exhausted" and issued ZERO expansion queries — the Codex/Fable P1. When R2 is
607	    # OFF the full angle frontier is seeded (the R1-only path is unchanged in count).
608	    _r2_on = _fc.facet_completeness_enabled()
609	    _seed_angle_limit = _seed_angles_per_facet() if _r2_on else None
610	    seed_queries = _breadth_first_seeds(facets, _seed_angle_limit)
611	
612	    # S2 (planning gate): PREPEND the gate projection's scope-anchored amplified
613	    # queries to the facet frontier and RAISE the budget by the net-new gate slice
614	    # — exactly like the sub_entity / landmark widen lanes — so a gate lane
615	    # (journal-only / English / a mandatory topic) is issued from the FIRST query
616	    # and NEVER swaps out a baseline facet query. This is the no-starvation core:
617	    # the gate's scope reaches the seed frontier BEFORE any fetch, additively. []
618	    # (default / OFF / no projection) => byte-identical facet frontier.
619	    _gate_qs = _projection_amplified_queries(retrieval_plan, question)
620	    if _gate_qs:
621	        _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
622	        _new_gate = [q for q in _gate_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
623	        if _new_gate:
624	            # gate lanes lead; budget rises by the net-new slice (never displaces).
625	            seed_queries = list(_new_gate) + list(seed_queries)
626	            max_queries = max_queries + len(_new_gate)
627	        logger.info(
628	            "[activation] planning_gate_projection: amplified_queries=%d", len(_new_gate)
629	        )
630	
631	    # R5 (I-deepfix-001, #1344): multilingual / cross-lingual frontier. On a
632	    # non-English (e.g. zh) DRB-II task the English facet queries never reach the
633	    # native-language primaries; detect the task's language profile and ADD
634	    # on-language queries (English stays first + unchanged) so a native-language
635	    # source and its English paraphrase land in the SAME multi-backend retrieval,
636	    # cross-lingually reranked into one consolidation basket. English-only tasks
637	    # are byte-identical (the expansion returns the seeds unchanged); default-ON,
638	    # OFF via PG_MULTILINGUAL_RETRIEVAL. §-1.3: adds on-language queries only,
639	    # drops ZERO sources, touches no faithfulness gate.
640	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import language_profile as _lp
641	    if _lp.multilingual_enabled():
642	        _profile = _lp.detect_language_profile(question)
643	        if _profile.is_multilingual:
644	            # Inject a production translator wrapping the GLM policy so an EXPLICIT
645	            # -language task (all-ASCII English body + "Answer in Chinese") — which has
646	            # no native script to carry — still emits real native-language queries.
647	            _translate_fn = _make_multilingual_translate_fn(
648	                llm, list(seed_queries), _profile.non_english
649	            )
650	            _expanded = _lp.expand_queries_for_profile(
651	                seed_queries, _profile, question, translate_fn=_translate_fn
652	            )
653	            # Separate the native additions from the English seeds, then RESERVE a
654	            # bounded slice of native queries inside the query budget so a wide English
655	            # R1 frontier cannot starve the task's own language out of the issued set.
656	            _eng_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
657	            _native_adds = [
658	                q for q in _expanded if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _eng_keys
659	            ]
660	            seed_queries = _reserve_native_within_budget(
661	                list(seed_queries), _native_adds, max_queries
662	            )
663	
664	    # R2 entity-qgen (I-deepfix-001, #1344): STORM-style sub-entity + perspective query expansion.
665	    # The abstract facet frontier names sub-TOPICS x analytical angles but never the concrete NICHE
666	    # sub-entities a DRB-II rubric names (specific occupations / sectors / demographic groups), so
667	    # their profession-specific primaries are never fetched and the corpus stays canonical-only. This
668	    # AUGMENTS (does not replace) the frontier: ONE bounded LLM call enumerates the named sub-entities
669	    # + deterministic STORM-style disciplinary perspective lenses, each scope-anchored to the question
670	    # so it cannot drift off-subject, and the bounded sub-entity slice is ADDED ON TOP of the FULL
671	    # baseline frontier — the effective query budget is RAISED by the added slice so the sub-entity
672	    # queries never SWAP OUT a baseline query (the Codex/Fable iter-1 REVISE). Every discovered source
673	    # flows through the UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve + frozen faithfulness engine — NEVER auto-trusted.
674	    # Default OFF (PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION) => byte-identical. §-1.3: adds on-topic queries only,
675	    # drops ZERO sources, touches no faithfulness gate.
676	    #
677	    # Placed AFTER the R5 block (Codex/Fable iter-2 P1 fix). When R2 ran BEFORE R5 it lengthened
678	    # `seed_queries` first, and `language_profile.expand_queries_for_profile` caps its native-language
679	    # additions at max(PG_MULTILINGUAL_MAX_QUERIES, len(base)); once the R2 slice pushed `base` over
680	    # that cap the native-language queries a flag-OFF run WOULD issue were DROPPED — breaking the §-1.3
681	    # strict-superset on non-English (e.g. zh) tasks. Running R5 first means R5 expands the PRE-R2
682	    # baseline — the IDENTICAL input the flag-OFF path expands — into `seed_queries` (English + reserved
683	    # native). `widen_with_sub_entities` then keeps that whole R5-expanded window at the front and only
684	    # RAISES the budget by the sub-entity slice, so the flag-ON issued set is a strict SUPERSET of the
685	    # flag-OFF set: every native-language query is STILL issued (zero dropped) AND the sub-entity queries
686	    # are added. The sub-entity queries are English scope-anchored and route through the unchanged fetch
687	    # un-translated — option-(b): R5 native widening + R2 sub-entity widening both land on the budget;
688	    # translating the sub-entity slice is deliberately NOT done (it would complicate the superset for no
689	    # required gain). Dedup is against the CURRENT (R5-expanded) frontier so no query is issued twice.
690	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import sub_entity_query_expander as _sqe
691	    if _sqe.sub_entity_expansion_enabled() and not _wall_passed():
692	        _sub_qs = _sqe.plan_sub_entity_queries(question, llm)
693	        _new_sub_count = 0
694	        if _sub_qs:
695	            _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
696	            _new_sub = [q for q in _sub_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
697	            _new_sub_count = len(_new_sub)
698	            if _new_sub:
699	                seed_queries, max_queries = _sqe.widen_with_sub_entities(
700	                    list(seed_queries), _new_sub, max_queries
701	                )
702	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): sub-entity-expansion ACTIVATION fire marker. Emitted ONLY inside
703	        # this PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION-gated block => OFF byte-identical. expanded_queries = the NET
704	        # NEW sub-entity queries added on top of the current frontier (0 = LLM named none / all duplicates,
705	        # the eligible-yet-zero signal). Structural presence + count (§-1.3).
706	        logger.info("[activation] subentity_query_expansion: expanded_queries=%d", _new_sub_count)
707	
708	    # COV-C landmark-study expander (I-deepfix-001 Wave-3, #1344): the abstract facet + sub-entity
709	    # frontier never names the LANDMARK empirical studies / RCTs / seminal datasets central to the
710	    # question (the question carries no author names), so the empirical CORE stays absent. ONE bounded
711	    # LLM call enumerates the IN-WINDOW landmark studies (constrained to the question's stated
712	    # publication ceiling `date_end_iso` so a "before June 2023" question seeds the pre-print /

1096	    sources: list = []
1097	    seen_src: set[str] = set()
1098	    api_calls: dict[str, int] = {}
1099	    notes: list[str] = []
1100	    sidecar: dict = {}
1101	    corpus_truncated = False
1102	    # I-deepfix-001 P1-2 / P1-4 (#1344): carry the retrieval-wall + B4 semantic
1103	    # fallback disclosure across the FS-Researcher merge so the winner-firing gate
1104	    # and the manifest/report see them (OR-combine the booleans like corpus_truncated;
1105	    # SUM the per-round counts like the other candidate counts).
1106	    retrieval_wall_hit = False
1107	    semantic_relevance_fell_back = False
1108	    retrieval_queries_skipped = 0
1109	    retrieval_candidates_unclassified = 0
1110	    pre = kept_scope = kept_off = fetched = failed = 0
1111	    cand_total = cand_processed = 0
1112	    for r in results:
1113	        for row in getattr(r, "evidence_rows", None) or []:
1114	            url = (row.get("source_url") or "").strip()
1115	            if url and url in seen_src_for_ev:  # dedup the same source across rounds
1116	                continue
1117	            if url:
1118	                seen_src_for_ev.add(url)
1119	            new_row = dict(row)
1120	            new_row["evidence_id"] = f"ev_{len(ev_rows):03d}"  # RENUMBER globally-unique
1121	            ev_rows.append(new_row)
1122	        for src in getattr(r, "classified_sources", None) or []:
1123	            url = (getattr(src, "url", "") or "").strip()
1124	            if url and url in seen_src:
1125	                continue
1126	            if url:
1127	                seen_src.add(url)
1128	            sources.append(src)
1129	        for k, v in (getattr(r, "api_calls", None) or {}).items():
1130	            api_calls[k] = api_calls.get(k, 0) + int(v)
1131	        notes.extend(getattr(r, "notes", None) or [])
1132	        _sc = getattr(r, "journal_metadata_sidecar", None)
1133	        if isinstance(_sc, dict):
1134	            sidecar.update(_sc)  # keyed by canonical URL -> merge keeps all rounds' entries
1135	        if getattr(r, "corpus_truncated", False):
1136	            corpus_truncated = True
1137	        if getattr(r, "retrieval_wall_hit", False):
1138	            retrieval_wall_hit = True
1139	        if getattr(r, "semantic_relevance_fell_back", False):
1140	            semantic_relevance_fell_back = True
1141	        retrieval_queries_skipped += int(getattr(r, "retrieval_queries_skipped", 0) or 0)
1142	        retrieval_candidates_unclassified += int(
1143	            getattr(r, "retrieval_candidates_unclassified", 0) or 0
1144	        )
1145	        pre += int(getattr(r, "total_candidates_pre_filter", 0) or 0)
1146	        kept_scope += int(getattr(r, "candidates_kept_by_scope", 0) or 0)
1147	        kept_off += int(getattr(r, "candidates_kept_by_offtopic", 0) or 0)
1148	        fetched += int(getattr(r, "candidates_fetched", 0) or 0)
1149	        failed += int(getattr(r, "candidates_failed_fetch", 0) or 0)
1150	        cand_total += int(getattr(r, "candidates_total", 0) or 0)
1151	        cand_processed += int(getattr(r, "candidates_processed", 0) or 0)
1152	    notes.append(f"fs_researcher: merged {len(results)} queries -> {len(ev_rows)} evidence rows (renumbered)")
1153	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344, winner-gate false-negative): carry the W5 content-relevance
1154	    # telemetry through the merge. The per-query reports were DROPPED here (the I-deepfix-001
1155	    # P1-2/P1-4 carry-through wired retrieval_wall_hit + semantic_relevance_fell_back but MISSED
1156	    # this one) -> winner_firing_gate read retrieval.content_relevance=None and false-aborted
1157	    # "the judge never ran" even though the reranker FIRED every round (scored/demoted real
1158	    # passages). Carry a report whose reranker actually LOADED (reranker_device != 'unavailable'),
1159	    # preferring the most comprehensive round (largest n_scored); if EVERY round's reranker failed
1160	    # to load, carry an 'unavailable' report so the gate correctly marks W5 dark; None iff no round
1161	    # produced a report. Telemetry/disclosure only — faithfulness-NEUTRAL (content_relevance is a
1162	    # WEIGHT, never the faithfulness engine; §-1.3 no drop).
1163	    _cr_reports = [
1164	        c for c in (getattr(r, "content_relevance", None) for r in results)
1165	        if isinstance(c, dict)
1166	    ]
1167	    _cr_loaded = [
1168	        c for c in _cr_reports
1169	        if str(c.get("reranker_device", "") or "").strip().lower() != "unavailable"
1170	    ]
1171	    merged_content_relevance = (
1172	        max(_cr_loaded, key=lambda c: int(c.get("n_scored", 0) or 0)) if _cr_loaded
1173	        else (_cr_reports[0] if _cr_reports else None)
1174	    )
1175	    return result_factory(
1176	        classified_sources=sources, evidence_rows=ev_rows, total_candidates_pre_filter=pre,
1177	        candidates_kept_by_scope=kept_scope, candidates_kept_by_offtopic=kept_off,
1178	        candidates_fetched=fetched, candidates_failed_fetch=failed, api_calls=api_calls, notes=notes,
1179	        corpus_truncated=corpus_truncated, candidates_total=cand_total,
1180	        candidates_processed=cand_processed,
1181	        journal_metadata_sidecar=(sidecar or None),
1182	        # I-deepfix-001 P1-2 / P1-4 (#1344): merged retrieval-wall + B4 fallback disclosure.
1183	        retrieval_wall_hit=retrieval_wall_hit,
1184	        retrieval_queries_skipped=retrieval_queries_skipped,
1185	        retrieval_candidates_unclassified=retrieval_candidates_unclassified,
1186	        semantic_relevance_fell_back=semantic_relevance_fell_back,
1187	        # I-deepfix-001 (#1344): carry the merged W5 content-relevance telemetry so the
1188	        # winner-firing gate sees the reranker fired (was dropped -> false-abort).
1189	        content_relevance=merged_content_relevance,
1190	    )
1191	
1192	
1193	def run_fs_researcher_retrieval(
1194	    question: str,
1195	    llm: LlmFn,
1196	    per_query_retrieve: PerQueryRetrieveFn,
1197	    result_factory: Callable[..., Any],
1198	    *,
1199	    max_queries: int | None = None,
1200	    max_rounds: int | None = None,
1201	    retrieve_kwargs: dict | None = None,
1202	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: "float | None" = None,
1203	    retrieval_plan: Any = None,
1204	) -> tuple[Any, list[str]]:
1205	    """The production entry point: run the FS-Researcher loop over `per_query_retrieve` and return
1206	    (merged LiveRetrievalResult, queries_issued). Faithful to the bake-off winner — each query goes
1207	    through the SAME production retrieval; only query SELECTION is FS-Researcher.
1208	
1209	    I-deepfix-001 WALL-03 (#1344): ``retrieval_deadline_monotonic`` is the SHARED per-question
1210	    retrieval wall threaded from the spine; the adaptive GLM rounds stop firing once it passes
1211	    (``None`` = default = byte-identical).
1212	
1213	    S2 (planning gate): ``retrieval_plan`` is an optional
1214	    ``planning.retrieval_projection.RetrievalProjection``. When set (PG_GATE ON),
1215	    its mandatory-intent + entity + scope-anchored amplified queries are PREPENDED
1216	    to the FS frontier so the gate's scope (journal-only / English / each mandatory
1217	    topic) shapes discovery from the FIRST query — never a post-fetch filter.
1218	    ``None`` (default) => byte-identical champion behaviour."""
1219	    queries, results = plan_fs_researcher_queries(
1220	        question, llm, per_query_retrieve,
1221	        max_queries=max_queries, max_rounds=max_rounds, retrieve_kwargs=retrieve_kwargs,
1222	        retrieval_deadline_monotonic=retrieval_deadline_monotonic,
1223	        retrieval_plan=retrieval_plan,
1224	    )
1225	    return merge_retrieval_results(results, result_factory), queries

1	"""M-61 (2026-04-23): V30 hybrid human/licensed completion (Path B).
2	
3	V30 Report Contract Architecture Layer 4d. Codex plan review #6:
4	  "replace free-text-only `consent_proof` with a structured
5	   provenance object. Minimum fields: curator_id, source_type,
6	   source_locator (DOI + page range or equivalent), acquired_at,
7	   artifact_sha256 of the retained PDF/page image/snippet,
8	   artifact_retention_path or audit pointer, quote_page_range,
9	   attestation."
10	
11	M-61 accepts operator-provided direct_quote content for entities
12	that M-56 couldn't retrieve (paywalled-no-OA-no-abstract). The
13	completion file schema REQUIRES structured provenance, not just
14	a free-text consent string, so a fabricated quote cannot
15	self-consistently pass without retained-artifact evidence.
16	
17	## Two-sided interface
18	
19	Input 1 (from M-60): human_gap_tasks.json — list of tasks per
20	curator-actionable gap entry (what M-60.compose_human_completion_tasks
21	emits).
22	
23	Input 2 (operator-authored): human_gap_completions.json — list
24	of HumanCompletionRecord objects matching the task list.
25	
26	M-61:
27	  load_completions(path) → tuple[HumanCompletionRecord, ...]
28	  validate_against_tasks(completions, tasks) →
29	    (accepted, rejected: list[tuple[record, reason]])
30	  to_frame_rows(accepted, contract) → tuple[FrameRow, ...]
31	    — produces provenance_class=HUMAN_CURATED FrameRow objects
32	    that integrate with the rest of the pipeline as if M-56 had
33	    retrieved them, but with the permanent human_curated flag.
34	
35	## Fraud/fabrication defense
36	
37	Per Codex review #6: `strict_verify` cannot detect a fabricated
38	quote that self-consistently matches its claimed source_span.
39	Defense-in-depth:
40	
41	  1. artifact_sha256 required — references a retained file on
42	     the operator's system that can be independently recomputed
43	     and compared at audit time.
44	  2. artifact_retention_path required — where the retained PDF
45	     / page image / text snippet lives for audit retrieval.
46	  3. curator_id required — who asserted this.
47	  4. acquired_at required — when (ISO-8601).
48	  5. attestation required — curator's signed statement of
49	     licensed access.
50	  6. doi match with the task required — operator cannot
51	     substitute content from a different paper.
52	  7. provenance_class=HUMAN_CURATED is a PERMANENT flag on the
53	     FrameRow. Downstream rendering MUST surface this in the
54	     report's Methods section and in every citation marker.
55	
56	## Entity-type-agnostic (Codex rev #7)
57	
58	No branching on entity_type. statute / dft_primary / any domain
59	works — the completion schema is the same.
60	
61	## Pure functions
62	
63	All M-61 public functions are pure given their JSON / dict
64	inputs. I/O (reading completion files, writing FrameRows to
65	evidence pool) happens at the integration layer.
66	"""

80	class CompletionSchemaError(ValueError):
81	    """Raised when a completion record is malformed or missing
82	    required provenance fields."""
83	
84	    def __init__(self, record_index: int, reason: str) -> None:
85	        super().__init__(
86	            f"completion record [{record_index}] schema error: "
87	            f"{reason}"
88	        )
89	        self.record_index = record_index
90	        self.reason = reason

93	@dataclass(frozen=True)
94	class StructuredProvenance:
95	    """Codex plan review #6 minimum fields for human-curated
96	    provenance. All fields required — no free-text fallback.
97	
98	    Field definitions:
99	
100	    curator_id: opaque identifier for who asserted this. Required
101	      so the audit log can trace every human-curated row to a
102	      curator account.
103	    source_type: one of 'licensed_institutional_access' |
104	      'licensed_personal_subscription' | 'author_communication' |
105	      'pre-print_accessed_legally' | 'other'. Limited vocabulary.
106	    source_locator: DOI + page range (e.g. "10.1016/S0140-6736(21)01443-4 pp.1811-1824"),
107	      or equivalent stable reference (PMC ID, URL at permanent
108	      archive, etc). Must uniquely identify the cited passage.
109	    acquired_at: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when operator accessed the
110	      source. Must be timezone-aware AND offset=UTC (+00:00 or 'Z').
111	      Codex M-61 audit Nit: non-UTC offsets were silently accepted.
112	    artifact_sha256: hex digest of the retained file (PDF page
113	      image / text snippet / screenshot). Independently
114	      recomputable at audit time. Hex chars only, 64 long.
115	    artifact_retention_path: pointer to where the retained file
116	      lives — operator's audit share, institutional repository,
117	      etc. Not a public URL.
118	    quote_page_range: "p.1811" or "pp.1811-1812" style citation
119	      within the source. Allows audit to locate the exact passage.
120	    attestation: curator's signed statement of licensed access.
121	      Free-text but structured — "I hereby certify that ..."
122	      form. Audit-logged.
123	    other_justification: required IFF source_type='other'. Codex
124	      M-61 audit Medium: the `other` pressure valve needs a
125	      justification so it doesn't become an unaudited bucket.
126	    """
127	
128	    curator_id: str
129	    source_type: str
130	    source_locator: str
131	    acquired_at: str                    # ISO-8601 UTC
132	    artifact_sha256: str                # 64 hex chars
133	    artifact_retention_path: str
134	    quote_page_range: str
135	    attestation: str
136	    other_justification: str | None = None
137	
138	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, str]:
139	        """Serialize to plain dict for FrameRow.human_curated_provenance
140	        threading (avoids circular import between frame_fetcher
141	        and human_gap_completion)."""
142	        out = {
143	            "curator_id": self.curator_id,
144	            "source_type": self.source_type,
145	            "source_locator": self.source_locator,
146	            "acquired_at": self.acquired_at,
147	            "artifact_sha256": self.artifact_sha256,
148	            "artifact_retention_path": self.artifact_retention_path,
149	            "quote_page_range": self.quote_page_range,
150	            "attestation": self.attestation,
151	        }
152	        if self.other_justification is not None:
153	            out["other_justification"] = self.other_justification
154	        return out

157	@dataclass(frozen=True)
158	class HumanCompletionRecord:
159	    """One operator-provided completion for a gap entity."""
160	
161	    entity_id: str
162	    doi: str | None                     # may be None for entities
163	                                        # without DOI (statute, URL-only)
164	    direct_quote: str
165	    provenance: StructuredProvenance
166	    # Optional echo of source_span the operator believes backs the
167	    # quote; M-58 anti-fabrication check still enforces value == span
168	    # at slot-fill time. Absent = the whole direct_quote is the span.

172	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	# Validation
174	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
175	_ALLOWED_SOURCE_TYPES = frozenset({
176	    "licensed_institutional_access",
177	    "licensed_personal_subscription",
178	    "author_communication",
179	    "pre-print_accessed_legally",
180	    "other",
181	})
182	
183	_SHA256_RE = re.compile(r"^[0-9a-f]{64}$")
184	
185	# Codex M-61 audit Medium: the schema must be closed against
186	# legacy free-text channels. An `other` source_type must carry
187	# a justification field so the allowlist's pressure valve doesn't
188	# become an unaudited bucket. Legacy `consent_proof` key is
189	# explicitly rejected at parse.
190	_ALLOWED_COMPLETION_KEYS = frozenset({
191	    "entity_id", "doi", "direct_quote", "source_span", "provenance",
192	})
193	_ALLOWED_PROVENANCE_KEYS_BASE = frozenset({
194	    "curator_id", "source_type", "source_locator", "acquired_at",
195	    "artifact_sha256", "artifact_retention_path",
196	    "quote_page_range", "attestation",
197	})
198	# Extra key permitted ONLY when source_type="other".
199	_OTHER_JUSTIFICATION_KEY = "other_justification"

202	def parse_completion(
203	    raw: dict[str, Any], record_index: int,
204	) -> HumanCompletionRecord:
205	    """Parse one raw JSON record into HumanCompletionRecord.
206	
207	    Codex M-61 audit Medium: schema is now CLOSED against legacy
208	    free-text channels. Any key outside _ALLOWED_COMPLETION_KEYS
209	    (e.g. legacy `consent_proof`) raises CompletionSchemaError.
210	
211	    Raises CompletionSchemaError with record_index on any
212	    malformation, missing required provenance field, or
213	    unknown key.
214	    """
215	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
216	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
217	            record_index, f"expected dict, got {type(raw).__name__}"
218	        )
219	
220	    # Codex M-61 audit Medium: explicit unknown-key rejection.
221	    unknown = set(raw.keys()) - _ALLOWED_COMPLETION_KEYS
222	    if unknown:
223	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
224	            record_index,
225	            f"unknown keys: {sorted(unknown)}. Legacy free-text "
226	            f"channels (e.g. 'consent_proof') are rejected — "
227	            f"use the structured `provenance` object.",
228	        )
229	
230	    entity_id = raw.get("entity_id")
231	    if not isinstance(entity_id, str) or not entity_id.strip():
232	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
233	            record_index, "entity_id must be non-empty string"
234	        )
235	
236	    doi = raw.get("doi")
237	    if doi is not None and not isinstance(doi, str):
238	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
239	            record_index,
240	            f"doi must be str or null, got {type(doi).__name__}",
241	        )
242	
243	    direct_quote = raw.get("direct_quote")
244	    if not isinstance(direct_quote, str) or not direct_quote.strip():
245	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
246	            record_index, "direct_quote must be non-empty string"
247	        )
248	
249	    source_span = raw.get("source_span")
250	    if source_span is not None and not isinstance(source_span, str):
251	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
252	            record_index,
253	            f"source_span must be str or null, got "
254	            f"{type(source_span).__name__}",
255	        )
256	
257	    provenance_raw = raw.get("provenance")
258	    if not isinstance(provenance_raw, dict):
259	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
260	            record_index,
261	            "provenance object required (structured per Codex plan "
262	            "review #6) — free-text consent_proof no longer "
263	            "accepted",
264	        )
265	    provenance = _parse_provenance(provenance_raw, record_index)
266	
267	    return HumanCompletionRecord(
268	        entity_id=entity_id,
269	        doi=doi if doi and doi.strip() else None,
270	        direct_quote=direct_quote,
271	        provenance=provenance,
272	        source_span=(
273	            source_span if source_span and source_span.strip()
274	            else None
275	        ),
276	    )

278	
279	def _parse_provenance(
280	    raw: dict[str, Any], record_index: int,
281	) -> StructuredProvenance:
282	    # Codex M-61 audit: unknown-key rejection plus conditional
283	    # other_justification enforcement.
284	    allowed_keys = set(_ALLOWED_PROVENANCE_KEYS_BASE)
285	    source_type_raw = raw.get("source_type")
286	    if source_type_raw == "other":
287	        allowed_keys.add(_OTHER_JUSTIFICATION_KEY)
288	    unknown = set(raw.keys()) - allowed_keys
289	    if unknown:
290	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
291	            record_index,
292	            f"provenance unknown keys: {sorted(unknown)}",
293	        )
294	
295	    required = set(_ALLOWED_PROVENANCE_KEYS_BASE)
296	    missing = required - set(raw.keys())
297	    if missing:
298	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
299	            record_index,
300	            f"provenance missing required fields: {sorted(missing)}",
301	        )
302	    for key in required:
303	        value = raw[key]
304	        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
305	            raise CompletionSchemaError(
306	                record_index,
307	                f"provenance.{key} must be non-empty string",
308	            )
309	
310	    source_type = raw["source_type"]
311	    if source_type not in _ALLOWED_SOURCE_TYPES:
312	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
313	            record_index,
314	            f"provenance.source_type={source_type!r} not in "
315	            f"allowed set {sorted(_ALLOWED_SOURCE_TYPES)}",
316	        )
317	
318	    # Codex M-61 audit Medium: source_type='other' MUST carry
319	    # other_justification. The pressure valve needs an audit hook.
320	    other_justification = None
321	    if source_type == "other":
322	        oj = raw.get(_OTHER_JUSTIFICATION_KEY)
323	        if not isinstance(oj, str) or not oj.strip():
324	            raise CompletionSchemaError(
325	                record_index,
326	                "provenance.other_justification required when "
327	                "source_type='other' (non-empty string)",
328	            )
329	        other_justification = oj
330	
331	    artifact_sha256 = raw["artifact_sha256"].lower()
332	    if not _SHA256_RE.match(artifact_sha256):
333	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
334	            record_index,
335	            "provenance.artifact_sha256 must be 64 hex chars "
336	            "(lowercase)",
337	        )
338	
339	    # Codex M-61 audit Nit: enforce UTC offset specifically, not
340	    # just timezone-awareness. Non-UTC offsets are rejected so the
341	    # audit timeline is consistent.
342	    acquired_at = raw["acquired_at"]
343	    try:
344	        normalized = acquired_at.replace("Z", "+00:00")
345	        dt = datetime.fromisoformat(normalized)
346	        if dt.tzinfo is None:
347	            raise ValueError("naive datetime not allowed")
348	        if dt.utcoffset() != timezone.utc.utcoffset(None):
349	            raise ValueError(
350	                f"timezone offset must be UTC; got "
351	                f"{dt.utcoffset()}"
352	            )
353	    except (ValueError, TypeError) as exc:
354	        raise CompletionSchemaError(
355	            record_index,
356	            f"provenance.acquired_at must be ISO-8601 UTC datetime "
357	            f"('+00:00' or 'Z'); got {acquired_at!r} ({exc})",
358	        ) from exc
359	
360	    return StructuredProvenance(
361	        curator_id=raw["curator_id"],
362	        source_type=source_type,
363	        source_locator=raw["source_locator"],
364	        acquired_at=acquired_at,
365	        artifact_sha256=artifact_sha256,
366	        artifact_retention_path=raw["artifact_retention_path"],
367	        quote_page_range=raw["quote_page_range"],
368	        attestation=raw["attestation"],
369	        other_justification=other_justification,
370	    )

373	def load_completions(
374	    path: str | Path,
375	) -> tuple[HumanCompletionRecord, ...]:
376	    """Load + parse a human_gap_completions.json file.
377	
378	    Returns tuple of parsed records in input order.
379	
380	    Raises:
381	        CompletionSchemaError: on any record-level malformation.
382	        FileNotFoundError: if the file doesn't exist.
383	        ValueError: if the file isn't a JSON array at the root.
384	    """
385	    p = Path(path)
386	    with p.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
387	        data = json.load(f)
388	    if not isinstance(data, list):
389	        raise ValueError(
390	            f"completion file {p} root must be JSON array, got "
391	            f"{type(data).__name__}"
392	        )
393	    return tuple(
394	        parse_completion(r, i) for i, r in enumerate(data)
395	    )
396	
397	
398	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
399	# Cross-check against M-60 task list
400	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
401	@dataclass(frozen=True)
402	class CompletionAcceptance:
403	    """Result of cross-checking completions against tasks."""
404	
405	    accepted: tuple[HumanCompletionRecord, ...]
406	    rejected: tuple[tuple[HumanCompletionRecord, str], ...]
407	
408	
409	def validate_against_tasks(
410	    completions: tuple[HumanCompletionRecord, ...],
411	    tasks: list[dict[str, Any]],
412	) -> CompletionAcceptance:
413	    """Cross-check each completion against the corresponding
414	    M-60 task. A completion is accepted iff:
415	
416	    1. entity_id matches a task in tasks[].
417	    2. DOI matches the task's doi EXACTLY — including DOI
418	       presence. If the task has a DOI, the completion MUST
419	       supply the same DOI. DOI omission is NOT a valid bypass
420	       (Codex M-61 audit Blocker 1).
421	    3. Only one completion per entity_id is accepted. Second
422	       and subsequent completions for the same entity are
423	       rejected (Codex M-61 audit Blocker 3 — prevents ambiguous
424	       audit trail).
425	
426	    Operator cannot:
427	      - substitute content from a different paper (DOI mismatch)
428	      - bypass the paper-binding by omitting DOI on their side
429	      - supply multiple completions for the same entity
430	      - supply content for an entity that isn't curator-actionable
431	    """
432	    tasks_by_eid: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {
433	        t["entity_id"]: t for t in tasks
434	    }
435	    accepted: list[HumanCompletionRecord] = []
436	    rejected: list[tuple[HumanCompletionRecord, str]] = []
437	    seen_entity_ids: set[str] = set()
438	    for c in completions:
439	        if c.entity_id in seen_entity_ids:
440	            rejected.append((
441	                c,
442	                f"duplicate completion for entity_id="
443	                f"{c.entity_id!r} — only one completion per "
444	                f"entity is accepted (audit integrity)",
445	            ))
446	            continue
447	        seen_entity_ids.add(c.entity_id)
448	
449	        task = tasks_by_eid.get(c.entity_id)
450	        if task is None:
451	            rejected.append((
452	                c,
453	                f"no task exists for entity_id={c.entity_id!r} — "
454	                f"operator cannot supply content for an entity "
455	                f"that's not curator-actionable",
456	            ))
457	            continue
458	        task_doi = task.get("doi")
459	        if task_doi:
460	            # Codex M-61 audit Blocker 1: task has a DOI →
461	            # completion MUST have the SAME DOI. None on the
462	            # completion side is no longer a free pass.
463	            if c.doi is None:
464	                rejected.append((
465	                    c,
466	                    f"completion.doi is None but task requires "
467	                    f"doi={task_doi!r} — operator cannot bypass "
468	                    f"paper-binding by omitting DOI",
469	                ))
470	                continue
471	            if task_doi != c.doi:
472	                rejected.append((
473	                    c,
474	                    f"doi mismatch: task.doi={task_doi!r} vs "
475	                    f"completion.doi={c.doi!r} — operator cannot "
476	                    f"substitute content from a different paper",
477	                ))
478	                continue
479	        else:
480	            # Task has no DOI (statute, URL-only regulatory) —
481	            # completion must also have no DOI, to prevent the
482	            # operator from claiming a DOI where the contract
483	            # doesn't have one.
484	            if c.doi is not None:
485	                rejected.append((
486	                    c,
487	                    f"task has no DOI but completion.doi="
488	                    f"{c.doi!r} — operator cannot add a DOI "
489	                    f"binding that the contract does not require",
490	                ))
491	                continue
492	        accepted.append(c)
493	
494	    return CompletionAcceptance(
495	        accepted=tuple(accepted),
496	        rejected=tuple(rejected),
497	    )
498	
499	
500	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
501	# Convert to FrameRow for pipeline integration
502	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
503	# Legacy string marker kept for back-compat with callers reading
504	# row.quote_source. The AUTHORITATIVE permanent flag is now
505	# ProvenanceClass.HUMAN_CURATED on row.provenance_class. Codex
506	# M-61 audit Blocker 2 fix.
507	HUMAN_CURATED_PROVENANCE = "human_curated"
508	
509	
510	def to_frame_rows(
511	    accepted: tuple[HumanCompletionRecord, ...],
512	    entity_metadata: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
513	) -> tuple[FrameRow, ...]:
514	    """Produce FrameRow objects for each accepted completion.
515	
516	    Codex M-61 audit Blocker 2 fix: provenance_class is now
517	    ProvenanceClass.HUMAN_CURATED (new enum value). Downstream
518	    layers that branch on provenance_class will see the permanent
519	    human-curated marker. Legacy quote_source="human_curated"
520	    kept for back-compat.
521	
522	    Codex M-61 audit Blocker 3 fix: structured provenance is now
523	    serialized into FrameRow.human_curated_provenance as a dict.
524	    Every field of StructuredProvenance survives the FrameRow
525	    boundary. Downstream manifest renderers can inline the
526	    audit evidence without needing an in-memory side channel.
527	
528	    `entity_metadata` is a map of entity_id → {rendering_slot,
529	    entity_type, ...} pulled from ReportContract.entities_by_id()
530	    at integration time. We don't import ReportContract here to
531	    keep M-61 independent of M-54 schema.
532	
533	    NOTE: M-58 SlotFillPayload + strict_verify still apply. A
534	    human-curated direct_quote must pass the same value==source_span
535	    verbatim check. The structured provenance is AUDIT evidence
536	    (independent of strict_verify) that the quote wasn't
537	    fabricated.
538	    """
539	    rows: list[FrameRow] = []
540	    for c in accepted:
541	        meta = entity_metadata.get(c.entity_id, {})
542	        rendering_slot = meta.get("rendering_slot", "")
543	        entity_type = meta.get("entity_type", "unknown")
544	        rows.append(FrameRow(
545	            entity_id=c.entity_id,
546	            entity_type=entity_type,
547	            rendering_slot=rendering_slot,
548	            provenance_class=ProvenanceClass.HUMAN_CURATED,
549	            direct_quote=c.direct_quote,
550	            quote_source=HUMAN_CURATED_PROVENANCE,
551	            doi=c.doi,
552	            pmid=None,
553	            oa_pdf_url=None,
554	            url=c.provenance.artifact_retention_path,
555	            title=None,
556	            authors=(),
557	            journal=None,
558	            year=None,
559	            failure_reason=None,
560	            retrieval_attempts=(),
561	            retrieval_timings=(),
562	            human_curated_provenance=c.provenance.to_dict(),
563	        ))
564	    return tuple(rows)
565	
566	
567	def compute_artifact_sha256(content: bytes) -> str:
568	    """Utility for operators: compute SHA-256 of a retained
569	    artifact to populate the `artifact_sha256` field. Same as
570	    hashlib.sha256(content).hexdigest() but wrapped so the
571	    canonical method is in one place."""
572	    return hashlib.sha256(content).hexdigest()
573	
574	
575	def compose_methods_disclosure_human_curated(
576	    n_retrieved: int, n_human_curated: int,
577	) -> str:
578	    """Methods-section snippet counting human-curated rows
579	    separately from retrieved rows. Per V30 plan: 'Tier
580	    disclosure ... counts human-curated rows separately from
581	    retrieved rows: "46 retrieved + 3 human-curated from
582	    licensed sources"'.
583	
584	    Codex M-61 audit Medium fix: say "licensed or attested
585	    sources" since allowed source_type values include
586	    author_communication and preprint and other-with-
587	    justification, not strictly subscription.
588	    """
589	    if n_human_curated == 0:
590	        return f"Evidence basis: {n_retrieved} retrieved rows, 0 human-curated."
591	    return (
592	        f"Evidence basis: {n_retrieved} retrieved + {n_human_curated} "
593	        f"human-curated from licensed or attested sources (see "
594	        f"manifest.json frame_coverage_report.entries with "
595	        f"provenance_class={ProvenanceClass.HUMAN_CURATED.value!r} "
596	        f"for curator attribution + artifact_sha256 + "
597	        f"retention pointer)."
598	    )
