169	def _enumerate_landmark_studies(
170	    question: str, llm: LlmFn, window_end: Optional[str], cap: int
171	) -> list[str]:
172	    """ONE bounded LOCKED-generator call: enumerate the landmark empirical studies the question implies.
173	
174	    Elicits the specific empirical studies / RCTs / seminal datasets (with author teams) most central
175	    to answering the question, constrained to the stated date window.
176	
177	    Failure semantics (I-deepfix-001 Wave-3b, #1344 — Codex P0 anti-dark false-green fix): a genuine
178	    ``llm(...)`` / planner EXCEPTION is NOT swallowed here — it PROPAGATES to the caller's fail-open
179	    handler (``fs_researcher_query_gen._plan_expert_facet_queries``) so the DISTINCT
180	    ``unavailable_failopen`` degrade marker fires and the run_gate_b activation canary REJECTS the dark
181	    lane. Swallowing the exception here (the prior ``except: return []``) made a real failure
182	    INDISTINGUISHABLE from a healthy RAN-ok-added-zero — the caller read ``[]`` and emitted a positive
183	    ``expanded_queries=0`` marker, the exact anti-dark false-green this wave targets. An empty /
184	    degenerate reply (the model legitimately naming no in-window study) still yields ``[]`` via
185	    ``_clean_study_lines`` — a RAN-ok-zero the caller reports as a healthy ``expanded_queries=0``
186	    (§-1.3: ran-ok-zero passes, NO count-threshold introduced). This only decides WHICH queries are
187	    searched — the faithfulness engine is never touched.
188	    """
189	    reply = llm(
190	        "You are an expert research librarian. List the LANDMARK empirical studies, randomized "
191	        "controlled trials, and seminal datasets (with their author teams) most central to "
192	        "answering the RESEARCH QUESTION below — the primary sources a systematic review of this "
193	        "question would be built on, which a generic keyword search would MISS because the "
194	        "question names no authors. Prefer the field-defining primaries over survey / opinion "
195	        "pieces. Every study MUST stay within the scope of the question.\n\n"
196	        + _window_clause(window_end)
197	        + "One study per line as 'author team, year, short title' — no numbering, no commentary.\n\n"
198	        "RESEARCH QUESTION:\n" + (question or "")
199	    )
200	    return _clean_study_lines(reply, cap)
201	
202	
203	def plan_landmark_study_queries(
204	    question: str, llm: LlmFn, window_end: Optional[str] = None
205	) -> list[str]:
206	    """Build the in-window landmark-study query frontier for ``question``.
207	
208	    Returns an ordered, de-duplicated list of scope-anchored queries (``{landmark study} {question
209	    anchor}``) — the empirical primaries a generic search misses — from ONE bounded LOCKED-generator
210	    call constrained to ``window_end``. Pure control flow over the injected ``llm`` — no network here.
211	    An empty enumeration returns ``[]`` (unlike the sub-entity widener there is no deterministic
212	    floor: a landmark query is only ever a real study name from the model, so nothing is emitted when
213	    the model returns nothing). The FS-Researcher ``_max_queries`` cap (compute-safety) still applies
214	    downstream, so this may return more than are ultimately issued — intentional headroom, never a
215	    forced count (§-1.3).
216	
217	    Failure semantics (I-deepfix-001 Wave-3b, #1344 — Codex P0): a genuine ``llm(...)`` / planner
218	    EXCEPTION from ``_enumerate_landmark_studies`` is NOT caught here — it PROPAGATES to the caller's
219	    fail-open handler so the ``unavailable_failopen`` degrade marker fires (a real failure must never
220	    read as a healthy ran-ok-added-zero). A legitimately empty model reply still returns ``[]`` (the
221	    accepted ran-ok-zero). ``_scope_anchor`` degrades internally to the question's own keywords and is
222	    never the source of a propagated failure.
223	    """
224	    anchor = _scope_anchor(question)
225	    out: list[str] = []
226	    seen: set[str] = set()
227	    for study in _enumerate_landmark_studies(question, llm, window_end, _max_landmark_studies()):
228	        q = _norm(f"{study} {anchor}")
229	        if not q:
230	            continue
231	        key = q.lower()
232	        if key in seen:
233	            continue
234	        seen.add(key)
235	        out.append(q)
236	    return out
237	
238	
239	def widen_with_landmark_studies(
240	    base_seeds: list[str],
241	    landmark_queries: list[str],
242	    max_queries: int,
243	) -> tuple[list[str], int]:
244	    """ADD the landmark-study queries ON TOP of the full baseline frontier and RAISE the effective
245	    query budget by exactly the added slice, so the flag-ON issued query set is a strict SUPERSET of
246	    the flag-OFF issued set.
247	
248	    Returns ``(widened_seed_queries, effective_max_queries)``.
249	
250	    Identical contract to ``sub_entity_query_expander.widen_with_sub_entities``: the flag-OFF path
251	    issues ``base_seeds[:max_queries]``; this keeps that exact baseline window at the FRONT in
252	    unchanged order, inserts a bounded slice of landmark queries immediately AFTER it, and returns a
253	    RAISED budget ``max_queries + reserve``. The downstream seed-issue loop (capped at the returned
254	    budget) therefore emits ``base_seeds[:max_queries] + reserved`` — every baseline query the flag-OFF
255	    path would issue is STILL issued, and the landmark queries are ADDED, never SWAPPED IN. Any
256	    baseline beyond the original budget and any leftover landmark queries trail after and issue only if
257	    the raised budget still has room — never displacing a baseline query. An empty ``landmark_queries``
258	    (or a reserve of 0) returns ``(base_seeds, max_queries)`` unchanged (byte-identical no-op).
259	
260	    §-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE widen-only: this ADDS retrieval by raising the budget by the bounded
261	    reserve (a LAW VI env cap, ``PG_LANDMARK_QUERY_RESERVE``); it introduces no cap / target / thinner,
262	    drops ZERO sources, and every added query still routes through the UNCHANGED ``per_query_retrieve``
263	    (scope -> tier -> fetch -> provenance) and the frozen faithfulness engine — a landmark source is
264	    NEVER auto-trusted.
265	    """
266	    if not landmark_queries:
267	        return list(base_seeds), max_queries
268	    reserve = min(len(landmark_queries), _landmark_reserve())
269	    if reserve <= 0:
270	        return list(base_seeds), max_queries
271	    reserved = landmark_queries[:reserve]
272	    leftover = landmark_queries[reserve:]
273	    baseline_window = base_seeds[:max_queries]  # exactly what the flag-OFF path issues
274	    baseline_tail = base_seeds[max_queries:]    # baseline beyond budget (neither path issues)
275	    widened = baseline_window + reserved + baseline_tail + leftover
276	    return widened, max_queries + reserve
277

1	"""Language-profile-driven multilingual / cross-lingual retrieval frontier.
2	
3	I-deepfix-001 R5 (#1344). DRB-II contains non-English (e.g. Chinese) tasks and
4	FORCES independent multi-source recall. Today the retrieval frontier is built
5	only from the question's own English words: ``query_decomposer`` and the
6	FS-Researcher facet planner emit English queries, the backends fan out English
7	strings, and on a zh task the highest-authority NATIVE-language primaries are
8	never queried — a real recall hole, not cosmetic. A competitor that queries in
9	the task language out-recalls POLARIS on those tasks.
10	
11	WHAT THIS MODULE DOES (pure, no-network, no-LLM by default)
12	===========================================================
13	1. :func:`detect_language_profile` — reads the task's language profile from the
14	   question text (Unicode script detection) plus any explicit "answer in <lang>"
15	   instruction. Deterministic; no model.
16	2. :func:`expand_queries_for_profile` — given the English facet/angle queries,
17	   ADDS on-language queries so a native-language source and its English paraphrase
18	   land in the SAME multi-backend retrieval (the already-multilingual Qwen3
19	   reranker then scores them cross-lingually into one consolidation basket). It
20	   carries the question's OWN native-script terms into the query fanout (an honest
21	   cross-lingual query — no fabricated translation) and, when the caller injects a
22	   ``translate_fn`` (production may wrap the GLM mirror), also appends true
23	   translations. English stays a query language, so **monolingual-English tasks
24	   are byte-identical** (the expansion returns the input queries unchanged).
25	
26	DNA (§-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE)
27	==================================
28	Pure frontier expansion into more languages. It ADDS on-language queries only and
29	DROPS ZERO sources; breadth emerges from facet x language coverage, never a
30	target. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / provenance) is
31	untouched — this only changes WHICH queries are issued. Flag-gated
32	``PG_MULTILINGUAL_RETRIEVAL`` (default ON); OFF or an English-only profile =>
33	byte-identical to today.
34	"""
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	import os
38	import re
39	import unicodedata
40	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
41	from typing import Callable, Optional, Sequence
42	
43	# (english_query, target_lang_code) -> translated query. Injected by production
44	# (may wrap the GLM mirror). None => deterministic native-term carry only.
45	TranslateFn = Callable[[str, str], str]
46	
47	_ON_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "on", "yes"})
48	
49	# Compute-safety cap on total emitted queries after multilingual expansion (never
50	# a breadth target — §-1.3; each query still flows the unchanged verify).
51	_MAX_QUERIES_ENV = "PG_MULTILINGUAL_MAX_QUERIES"
52	_DEFAULT_MAX_QUERIES = 40
53	
54	# Unicode-script -> ISO-639-1-ish language code for the scripts DRB-II tasks use.
55	# Script detection is deterministic and model-free; a script maps to its dominant
56	# task language for query-fanout purposes (Han -> zh, Hiragana/Katakana -> ja,
57	# Hangul -> ko, Cyrillic -> ru, Arabic -> ar). This is a RETRIEVAL routing hint,
58	# never a faithfulness signal.
59	_SCRIPT_LANG: tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (
60	    ("HIRAGANA", "ja"),
61	    ("KATAKANA", "ja"),
62	    ("HANGUL", "ko"),
63	    ("CJK", "zh"),  # CJK Unified Ideographs — shared Han; default zh for routing
64	    ("CYRILLIC", "ru"),
65	    ("ARABIC", "ar"),
66	)
67	
68	# Explicit language instructions in the question ("answer in Chinese", "用中文",
69	# "respond in Japanese"). Maps a lowercased language NAME to its code so an
70	# English-worded question that DEMANDS a non-English answer still routes native
71	# queries. Deterministic keyword map; not exhaustive by design (the script
72	# detector covers the common non-Latin cases).
73	_LANG_NAME_CODE: dict[str, str] = {
74	    "chinese": "zh",
75	    "mandarin": "zh",
76	    "中文": "zh",
77	    "japanese": "ja",
78	    "日本語": "ja",
79	    "korean": "ko",
80	    "한국어": "ko",
81	    "russian": "ru",
82	    "arabic": "ar",
83	    "french": "fr",
84	    "german": "de",
85	    "spanish": "es",
86	}
87	_INSTRUCTION_RE = re.compile(
88	    r"(?:answer|respond|reply|write|in)\s+(?:in\s+)?"
89	    r"(chinese|mandarin|japanese|korean|russian|arabic|french|german|spanish)",
90	    re.IGNORECASE,
91	)
92	
93	# A maximal run of native-script (non-ASCII-letter) characters — the question's
94	# own native terms, carried into the query fanout for an honest cross-lingual query.
95	_NATIVE_TERM_RE = re.compile(r"[^\x00-\x7f]+(?:\s+[^\x00-\x7f]+)*")
96	
97	
98	@dataclass(frozen=True)
99	class LanguageProfile:
100	    """The task's detected language profile for retrieval routing.
101	
102	    ``languages`` is the ordered, de-duplicated list of language codes the
103	    frontier should query in — ALWAYS including English (English stays a query
104	    language so monolingual-English recall is never lost). ``non_english`` are
105	    just the additional languages (empty for an English-only task).
106	    """
107	
108	    primary: str = "en"
109	    languages: tuple[str, ...] = ("en",)
110	    scripts: tuple[str, ...] = ()
111	
112	    @property
113	    def non_english(self) -> tuple[str, ...]:
114	        return tuple(code for code in self.languages if code != "en")
115	
116	    @property
117	    def is_multilingual(self) -> bool:
118	        return bool(self.non_english)
119	
120	
121	def multilingual_enabled() -> bool:
122	    """True iff `PG_MULTILINGUAL_RETRIEVAL` is truthy (default ON).
123	
124	    OFF => the expansion is a byte-identical no-op regardless of the profile.
125	    """
126	    return os.getenv("PG_MULTILINGUAL_RETRIEVAL", "1").strip().lower() in _ON_VALUES
127	
128	
129	def _max_queries() -> int:
130	    raw = os.getenv(_MAX_QUERIES_ENV, "").strip()
131	    if not raw:
132	        return _DEFAULT_MAX_QUERIES
133	    try:
134	        return max(1, int(raw))
135	    except ValueError:
136	        return _DEFAULT_MAX_QUERIES
137	
138	
139	def _char_script(ch: str) -> Optional[str]:
140	    """Return a coarse script bucket name for a character, or None for ASCII/marks."""
141	    try:
142	        name = unicodedata.name(ch)
143	    except ValueError:
144	        return None
145	    for token in ("HIRAGANA", "KATAKANA", "HANGUL", "CJK", "CYRILLIC", "ARABIC"):
146	        if token in name:
147	            return token
148	    return None
149	
150	
151	def detect_language_profile(
152	    question: str, *, instruction: Optional[str] = None
153	) -> LanguageProfile:
154	    """Detect the task language profile from the question text + any explicit
155	    language instruction. Pure / deterministic / no-LLM.
156	
157	    The profile carries EVERY language the frontier should query in — English
158	    always, plus any non-English language detected from (a) the non-Latin scripts
159	    present in the question body and (b) an explicit "answer in <language>"
160	    instruction. An all-ASCII question with no instruction => English-only
161	    (``is_multilingual`` False) => the expansion is a no-op.
162	    """
163	    text = question or ""
164	    scripts_found: list[str] = []
165	    langs: list[str] = ["en"]  # English is always a query language.
166	
167	    # (a) Script detection over the question body.
168	    seen_scripts: set[str] = set()
169	    for ch in text:
170	        bucket = _char_script(ch)
171	        if bucket and bucket not in seen_scripts:
172	            seen_scripts.add(bucket)
173	    for token, code in _SCRIPT_LANG:
174	        if token in seen_scripts:
175	            scripts_found.append(token)
176	            if code not in langs:
177	                langs.append(code)
178	
179	    # (b) Explicit "answer in <language>" instruction (in the question OR a
180	    # separate instruction string). Catches an English-worded question that
181	    # demands a non-English answer.
182	    hay = f"{text}\n{instruction or ''}"
183	    for m in _INSTRUCTION_RE.finditer(hay):
184	        code = _LANG_NAME_CODE.get(m.group(1).lower())
185	        if code and code not in langs:
186	            langs.append(code)
187	    for name, code in _LANG_NAME_CODE.items():
188	        if name in hay.lower() and code not in langs:
189	            langs.append(code)
190	
191	    # Primary = the first non-English language if any (the dominant native), else en.
192	    non_en = [c for c in langs if c != "en"]
193	    primary = non_en[0] if non_en else "en"
194	    return LanguageProfile(
195	        primary=primary,
196	        languages=tuple(langs),
197	        scripts=tuple(scripts_found),
198	    )
199	
200	
201	def native_terms(question: str) -> list[str]:
202	    """Extract the question's own native-script term runs (ordered, de-duplicated).
203	
204	    These are the honest cross-lingual seed: carrying the question's real
205	    native-language terms into the query fanout surfaces native-language sources
206	    WITHOUT fabricating a translation. Returns [] for an all-ASCII question.
207	    """
208	    out: list[str] = []
209	    seen: set[str] = set()
210	    for m in _NATIVE_TERM_RE.finditer(question or ""):
211	        term = " ".join(m.group(0).split()).strip()
212	        if term and term not in seen:
213	            seen.add(term)
214	            out.append(term)
215	    return out
216	
217	
218	def expand_queries_for_profile(
219	    queries: Sequence[str],
220	    profile: LanguageProfile,
221	    question: str,
222	    *,
223	    translate_fn: Optional[TranslateFn] = None,
224	    max_queries: Optional[int] = None,
225	) -> list[str]:
226	    """Add on-language queries for a multilingual profile; byte-identical for an
227	    English-only profile (or when the flag is OFF).
228	
229	    The English queries are preserved FIRST and UNCHANGED (English stays a query
230	    language). Then, for a multilingual profile:
231	
232	    * the question's own native-script terms are emitted as a standalone query,
233	      and prepended to each English query as a cross-lingual augmented query
234	      (honest — the terms are the question's real native words, no fabricated
235	      translation);
236	    * when ``translate_fn`` is injected (production may wrap the GLM mirror), a
237	      true translation of each English query into each non-English language is
238	      also appended.
239	
240	    All additions are de-duplicated (order-preserving) and the whole list is
241	    capped by `PG_MULTILINGUAL_MAX_QUERIES` (compute-safety, never a breadth
242	    target). DROPS ZERO input queries.
243	
244	    Args:
245	        queries: the English facet/angle queries.
246	        profile: the detected :class:`LanguageProfile`.
247	        question: the raw question (source of native-script terms).
248	        translate_fn: optional ``(query, lang) -> translated_query``.
249	        max_queries: override for `PG_MULTILINGUAL_MAX_QUERIES` (tests).
250	
251	    Returns:
252	        The expanded query list (English-first). Unchanged input list when the
253	        flag is OFF or the profile is English-only.
254	    """
255	    base = [q for q in (queries or []) if (q or "").strip()]
256	    if not multilingual_enabled() or not profile.is_multilingual:
257	        return list(base)
258	
259	    cap = _max_queries() if max_queries is None else max(1, int(max_queries))
260	    out: list[str] = []
261	    seen: set[str] = set()
262	
263	    def _dedup_clean(text: str) -> "str | None":
264	        cleaned = " ".join((text or "").split()).strip()
265	        if not cleaned:
266	            return None
267	        key = cleaned.lower()
268	        if key in seen:
269	            return None
270	        seen.add(key)
271	        return cleaned
272	
273	    # 1) English queries first, unchanged — NEVER dropped. The compute-safety cap applies
274	    # to the multilingual ADDITIONS only; truncating an input seed would contradict the
275	    # "DROPS ZERO input queries" contract (Fable P2). So every base query is emitted, and
276	    # the additions are bounded so the total never exceeds max(cap, len(base)).
277	    for q in base:
278	        cleaned = _dedup_clean(q)
279	        if cleaned is not None:
280	            out.append(cleaned)
281	
282	    # Additions ceiling: the compute cap, but never below the (fully-kept) base count.
283	    ceiling = max(cap, len(out))
284	
285	    def _add(text: str) -> bool:
286	        # Check the ceiling BEFORE appending: appending first (then testing) would
287	        # emit one extra multilingual addition whenever `out` is already AT the
288	        # ceiling — the off-by-one that let the total reach ceiling+1 (Codex P2).
289	        # A full frontier stops here without consuming `seen` or the input span.
290	        if len(out) >= ceiling:
291	            return False
292	        cleaned = _dedup_clean(text)
293	        if cleaned is None:
294	            return True
295	        out.append(cleaned)
296	        return len(out) < ceiling
297	
298	    terms = native_terms(question)
299	    native_phrase = " ".join(terms).strip()
300	
301	    # 2) The question's own native terms as a standalone cross-lingual query.
302	    if native_phrase and not _add(native_phrase):
303	        return out
304	
305	    # 3) Native-term-augmented cross-lingual variants of each English query.
306	    if native_phrase:
307	        for q in base:
308	            if not _add(f"{native_phrase} {q}"):
309	                return out
310	
311	    # 4) True translations when a translator is injected (production GLM mirror).
312	    if translate_fn is not None:
313	        for lang in profile.non_english:
314	            for q in base:
315	                try:
316	                    translated = translate_fn(q, lang)
317	                except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — translation is best-effort
318	                    continue
319	                if translated and not _add(translated):
320	                    return out
321	
322	    return out
323
